attributes.action: Restrict the Pay rise of the Members of N Ireland's Legislative Assembly (MLAs)
attributes.additional_details:
I am a Pensioner who has worked all my life, including 30 years in the N Ireland Prison Service, in a difficult, challenging and dangerous enviroment
To be told that the people of all the various political parties in my country will be receiving such a staggeringly large increase in pay, when our country is in such deep financial trouble, is not only alarming but sets the wrong president to other people. As a pensioner, my pay increase was 4.1 per cent, and my politicians should follow suit.
attributes.background:
Northern Ireland's Members of the Legislative Assembly are about to be awarded a £14.000 pay increase, which equals 27 per cent, of an increase 
I would respectfully request that this increase be limited to the UK's current rate of inflation, of between three and four per cent of a pay increase

rejection.details:
We can only accept petitions about things the UK Government or Parliament are directly responsible for.

Decisions about MLAs' pay are a matter for the Independent Remuneration Board, not the UK Government or Parliament.

You can find out about the board here: https://www.niassembly.gov.uk/your-mlas/members-salaries-and-expenses/independent-remuneration-board/
